name: Mycah Pittman
hs_athletic_background: Mycah Pittman | R-Jr. | WR | 5-11 | 205 | Tampa, Fla. | Calabasas High School (Calif.)/OregonAppeared in 22 games with 12 starts in three seasons at Oregon…totaled 38 receptions for 547 yards and two touchdowns in addition to 15 punt returns for 151 yards…caught 12 passes for 197 yards and registered 151 yards on 15 punt returns while appearing in 10 games with seven starts in 2021…started four of five games played in 2020, making eight catches for 123 yards…seven of eight catches his sophomore season resulted in first down…appeared in seven games his true freshman season, helping Oregon to 7-0 record in those games…registered 18 receptions for 227 yards and two touchdowns…made first collegiate start and had four catches for 30 yards in Rose Bowl win vs. Wisconsin…consensus four-star prospect in Class of 2019…ranked as No. 85 prospect in America, No. 13 wide receiver nationally and No. 16 prospect in California by 247Sports…recorded 177 receptions for 3,129 yards and 32 touchdowns with 11 100-yard receiving games and four with more than 200 yards in three high school seasons…two-time All-CIF Southern Section Division 2 first-team selection…made 67 catches for 828 yards and 12 touchdowns his senior year to help Coyotes advance to semifinal round of Division 2 Southern Section playoffs…totaled 1,027 receiving yards and 11 touchdowns on 60 receptions as a junior…played sophomore year at Oaks Christian and registered 55 catches for 1,329 yards and 10 touchdowns.

name: Khafre Brown
hs_athletic_background: At USF2023 (REDSHIRT SENIOR): Played in 12 games, six starts, with 27 receptions for 435 yards and five touchdowns … Caught three passes for a team-high 95 yards and a score in season opener vs. Western Kentucky (Sept. 2) … Scored his second touchdown in as many games with one of his three catches for 14 yards finding the end zone vs. Florida A&M (Sept. 9) … Hauled in a 59-yard pass in win over Rice (Sept. 23) … Caught a team-best seven passes for 80 yards in win at Navy (Sept. 30) … One catch for 25 yards at UAB (Oct. 7) … Returned to the lineup after missing two games to catch three passes for 32 yards and a touchdown at Memphis (Nov. 4) … Hauled in a 32-yard touchdown in win over Temple (Nov. 11) … Posted three catches for 32 yards at UTSA (Nov. 17) … Caught four passes for 38 yards in win over Charlotte (Nov. 25) … Recorded his fifth touchdown of the season with a 31-yard snag vs. Syracuse in the Boca Raton Bowl (Dec. 21).2022 (REDSHIRT JUNIOR): Played in nine games during his first season with the Bulls, catching four passes for 61 yards … Caught his first pass at USF vs. Howard (Sept. 10) for 19 yards … Hauled in one pass for a season-best 20 yards at Florida (Sept. 17) … Snagged one pass for 16 yards at Cincinnati (Oct. 8) … Caught his final pass of the season for six yards at Houston (Oct. 29).At North Carolina2021 (REDSHIRT SOPHOMORE): Played in six games, catching one pass for 75 yards in win over Virginia (Sept. 18).2020 (REDSHIRT FRESHMAN): Appeared in all 12 games as a redshirt freshman with two starts … Hauled in 15 passes for 337 yards and a pair of touchdowns … Averaged a team-best 22.5 yards per reception … Made first career start in season opener against Syracuse (Sept. 12) and responded with three catches for 35 yards … Scored his first career TD at Boston College (Oct. 3) on a 24-yard pass … Hauled in a 76-yard TD at Virginia (Oct. 31) … Had a career-high 87 receiving yards on three catches against Wake Forest (Nov. 14) … Came up with two catches for 40 yards in the Orange Bowl against Texas A&M (Jan. 2).2019 (FRESHMAN): Redshirted during his first season at North Carolina.PERSONAL: Son of Tamiko Patterson and Charles Brown … Younger brother, Dayami Brown, is a former North Carolina wide receiver and current member of the NFL’s Washington football team … Brown was named for the Egyptian king Khafre, who built the second largest pyramid of Giza during his 66-year reign, concluding somewhere around 2570 B.C. Brown’s mother was intrigued after seeing a picture of Khafre’s statue in a book … Brown paints for a hobby, specializing in sunsets, usually in a beach or skyline setting … He picked up the hobby from his mother, who is an artist … Pronounced CHA-free.

name: Langston Anderson
hs_athletic_background: Lots of history to go back through here and the most recent from 2018 has Anderson helping Heritage to an 11-2 season with 72 receptions for 1,291-yards and 18 touchdowns. He rushed 14 times for 156-yards and three touchdowns. He also had four kickoff returns for a 33.8-yards average. As a junior he earned honorable mention honors on the Texas Sportswriters Association Class 4A-Division I All-State team and was first-team All-District 6-4A-I as Heritage was 12-1 and Anderson had 55 catches for 860-yards and nine touchdowns. He rushed 14 times for 143-yards and a pair of touchdowns. His sophomore season he played at Jackson Academy in Flowood, Miss. and in a 6-6 season he had 51 receptions for 765-yards and six touchdowns. He ran for 32-yard on three carries. He returned 10 kickoffs for 203-yards with a longest return of 45-yards. On defense he had eight total tackles with five unassisted, a half sack, and six passes defended.Scouting Report: I used several playoff games for this and I’m going to start out with what you give, you get or what you reap, you sew. Meaning is Anderson will fit in nicely with the Oklahoma State receiver corps because he blocks. Anderson benefits plenty from teammates blocking for him because Heritage used him a lot on quick pass and screen pass concepts. It was important to see what he was doing when he was not the target and had blocking responsibilities and he was aggressive and showed good technique, he even had a few de-cleaters putting defenders on the turf. He has strong ball skills and catches the ball well, presents a good target. He has track, straight speed, but also in the quick and screen game shows the ability to make people miss in close quarters. He is used often in jet-action sweeps or flips and has good vision, several times turning the run up, even inside the tackles when it opened early. One good example was against Life Waxahachie on a scoring run inside the 10. He is an inside receiver with outside size and skill set. Route running (especially in situations where he is not targeted) will be his avenue to work on and improve along with overall work. Not the finished product, but he shouldn’t be, and he is an excellent talent has a chance to follow in the supreme line of Cowboys receivers.

name: Tyrone Broden
hs_athletic_background: 2023 (REDSHIRT SENIOR): Started four games and played in all 12 games in his first year at Arkansas, catching 15 passes for 109 yards and three touchdowns while also serving as a key member on special teams … Caught one pass for three yards in win over Western Carolina (Sept. 2) … Recorded the first rush of his career with a nine-yard run vs. BYU (Sept. 16) to go with one catch for 12 yards … Earned his first start and hauled in his first TD – a 19-yard catch – as a Hog at LSU (Sept. 23) as part of three catches for 31 yards … Caught two passes for 23 yards at Ole Miss (Oct. 7) … Posted two catches for 15 yards vs. Mississippi State (Oct. 21) … Snagged the game-winning touchdown in overtime victory at Florida (Nov. 4), caught three passes for 15 yards in the program’s first-ever win at Florida … One catch for six yards vs. Auburn (Nov. 11) and two catches for four yards vs. Florida International (Nov. 18), including a four-yard touchdown catch vs. FIU.2022 (REDSHIRT JUNIOR): Played in all 13 games, catching 32 passes for 506 yards and a team-high seven touchdowns … His 32 receptions ranked fourth on the team and 506 yards were second … Caught two passes for 14 yards in season opener at UCLA (9/3) … Recorded his first touchdown of the season with a 20-yard reception against Eastern Kentucky (9/10) … Snagged a pair of passes for 34 yards, including a 28-yarder, vs. Marshall (9/17) … At Mississippi State (9/24), caught two passes for 30 yards and a score .. Hauled in a 44-yard reception as one of three catches on the day for 61 yards at Akron (10/1) … Caught two passes for 39 yards vs. Buffalo (10/8) … Three catches for 42 yards against Miami (OH) (10/15) … Made a significant impact at Central Michigan (10/22) with a season-high two touchdowns as part of a three-catch, 53-yard performance … Registered 15 yards on two receptions in first MACtion game of the season vs. Western Michigan (11/2) to become the 40th player in program history to record 1,000 yards receiving in his career … Recorded the best performance of his career with eight catches for 84 yards and a TD vs. Kent State (11/9) … Proved to be a deep threat at Ohio (11/22) with 81 yards on just two catches, including a 44-yard touchdown in the first quarter … Caught a fourth quarter touchdown pass in Quick Lane Bowl vs. New Mexico State (12/26).2021 (REDSHIRT SOPHOMORE): Emerged as a playmaker for the Falcons in his third year, playing in 11 games – nine starts – and hauling in 36 passes for 596 yards and five touchdowns … His 596 yards receiving and five touchdown catches led the Falcons with his 35 receptions ranking third … Caught three passes for a team-high 76 yards in his season debut vs. South Alabama (9/11) … Followed up with a season-best six receptions for 52 yards in win over Murray State (9/18) … Caught four passes for 44 yards at Minnesota (9/25) … Found the end zone for the first time on a four-yard pass as part of a five-catch day that went for 75 yards at Kent State (10/2) … Recorded his first 100-yard game with 111 yards on just four catches with two going for scores vs. Akron (10/9) … Caught two passes for 53 yards, including a 44-yard pass, at Northern Illinois (10/16) … Registered three catches for 22 yards vs. Eastern Michigan (10/23) … Went over 100 yards receiving again in a victory at Buffalo (10/30) catching four passes for 110 yards and two TDs, including a 64-yard score to open the fourth quarter … Hauled in five passes for 53 yards at Miami (OH) (11/16) in his final game of the season.2020 (REDSHIRT FRESHMAN): Started all five games in pandemic-shortened season, catching six passes for 97 yards … Had one reception for 37 yards vs. Kent State (11/10) … Caught one pass for 13 yards against Buffalo (11/17) … Recorded a season-best two catches for 20 yards at Akron (12/5).2019 (FRESHMAN): Played in three games in his first year on campus while redshirting … Did not record any stats.HIGH SCHOOL: Helped lead West Bloomfield HS to a 9-3 mark as a senior … Listed as the No. 56 recruit on Detroit News’ Blue Chip List … Earned Oakland Activities Association Red All-League honors … Named Detroit News All-North Football Team honorable mention … A three-star recruit according to 247Sports and the No. 51 athlete in the state of Michigan.PERSONAL: Son of Tyrone Broden Sr. and Felicia Streater … Has two sisters – Tylicia and Tajanea Broden … Was the tallest wide receiver in the nation in 2022, sharing that distinction with three other players … TRANSFERRED TO ARKANSAS OVER: Colorado, Oklahoma, Oklahoma State, Penn State, Pittsburgh, Syracuse, Texas A&M, Washington State, among others.

100-plus game in season finale against Tulsa with 108 yards … Established career-highs in receptions against Rice and SMU (seven) … Recorded at least one reception in 11 of 12 games (multiple 10 times) … Targeted 84 time making 47 grabs (60.0 pct) … Stood second on the teal with 215 yards after the catch … Scored lone touchdown at UTSA (Oct. 28) on a four-yard pass from Alex Flinn with 15 seconds remaining in the contest … Registered 50 or more yards receiving in a game on six occasions … Made collegiate debut at No. 2 Michigan … First collegiate reception came against Marshall going for five yards … Forty of 47 receptions went for five or more yards (24 went for 10-plus) … Longest reception of the year was a 45-yard grab against SMU … Ten receptions went for 20 or more yards … Officially signed with East Carolina in May of 2023.2022 (Colorado): Played in three games during redshirt season before suffering injury … Caught two passes for 23 yards – both coming against TCU in the season opener … Career-long reception was 14 yards versus the Horn Frogs.High School: A three-star prospect by the recruiting services … 247 Composite listed him as the No. 168 wide receiver in the class, the No. 182 player from Texas and No. 1,249 player nationally … 247 Sports ranked him the No. 140 wide receiver nationally and No. 137 player from Texas … Played varsity for two seasons at Atascocita under coach Anson Carter … Senior season was a break-out season helping Atascocita to an 11-3 overall record and 5-1 record in district play … Finished his senior season with 70 catches for1,380 yards and 16 touchdowns … Added a punt return touchdown, a 68-yarder in a 52-0 win over Humble … Had seven catches for 182 yards and four touchdowns in a 41-20 win at Allen, marking the first home loss for Allen since Kyler Murray’s first start, some 84 games … Had seven catches for 135 yards and two touchdowns against Denton Guyer … Had six catches for 119 yards and a touchdown against North Shore in the playoffs … Also played baseball at Atascocita and was named first-team All-District 21-6A as a junior … An A-B honor roll student at Atascocita High School.Personal: Born in Gainesville, Fla. … Parents are Ronnie and Keisha Sowell … Had three siblings, Hunter, Ryleigh and Ryder … Dad Ronnie played college baseball at the University of Florida … Mom Keisha played college soccer at the University of Florida … Has two cousins who played college football and an uncle that played in the NFL … Last name is pronounced so-well … Majoring in communication.
